t: The Universal Autonomous Runner: Plan-Act-Reflect Loop That Never Goes Rogue
te: Drops any chat model into a disciplined goal -> plan -> act -> observe -> reflect loop with one action per turn, hard stop conditions, and an inspectable JSON state object every turn.

t: Goal Decomposer: Turn One Fuzzy Outcome Into an Executable Task Graph
te: Forces the agent to break a vague goal into a dependency-ordered DAG with tools, success checks, time estimates, and a flagged critical path BEFORE it touches anything.

t: AI Employee Onboarding: Build a Reusable Operator System Prompt in One Shot
te: Generates a complete, paste-ready system prompt that turns a model into a dependable 'AI employee' for one role - with SOPs, autonomy limits, a daily loop, persistent memory, and self-reported KPIs.

t: Multi-Agent Orchestrator: Manager Splits the Job, Parallel Sub-Agents Crush It
te: Stands up a manager-agent that decomposes a job, spawns specialized sub-agents to run independent threads in parallel, then synthesizes their outputs into one coherent deliverable - the orchestrator pattern that beats a single overloaded agent.

t: Reflexion Loop: Self-Critique Until Your Output Passes Its Own Rubric
te: Bolts a generate -> critique -> revise loop onto any task, with a harsh defect rubric, a carried-forward lessons note, and an iteration cap - the near-free 15-25% quality bump for autonomous output.

t: Grill-Me-First Agent: It Interviews You Before It Builds Anything
te: Inverts vibe-coding's failure mode - the agent relentlessly interviews you one question at a time (proposing its own recommended answer each time) until it can restate the full spec, then locks it before writing a line.

t: Schema-First Action Agent: Closed-Enum Tool Calls That Never Emit Bad JSON
te: Designs an autonomous agent that can only emit valid, schema-conformant JSON actions from a closed enum - the reliability pattern that makes invalid-JSON-rate a hard release gate.

t: Plan-and-Execute Agent: Strong Planner + Cheap Executor That Slashes Token Cost
te: Splits an autonomous job into a one-time strong-model plan and an N-step cheap-model executor with per-step verification - the token-economics pattern that beats pure ReAct on any multi-step task.

t: Deep-Research Agent: Reviewable Plan First, Then a Cited Autonomous Run
te: Turns a research question into a structured agent brief - sub-questions, source priorities, an output contract - proposes a plan you approve, then runs autonomously with inline citations, confidence levels, and flagged contradictions.

t: Human-in-the-Loop Safety Gate: No Irreversible Action Without Your Sign-Off
te: Wraps any agent in a defense-in-depth approval layer - it pauses for typed approval before any irreversible action, isolates untrusted tool output from its instructions, and flags prompt-injection attempts.

t: Evaluator-Optimizer Rig: A Strict Independent Grader Loops the Generator to a Pass
te: Builds a two-role autonomous quality loop - a Generator produces, a separate Evaluator who did NOT write the draft grades it against a rubric and returns pass/fail JSON until it passes or hits the cap. No rubber-stamping allowed.

t: Self-Maintaining Agent Memory: Save / Read / Forget Notes That Beat Context-Rot
te: Gives a long-running agent structured note-taking so it persists its plan and findings outside the context window - the fix for context-rot on multi-session autonomous work, with a cold-start handoff block.

t: Anti-Runaway Loop-Guard Designer: Stop Conditions That Kill Infinite Agent Loops
te: Hardens any autonomous agent against infinite loops, thrashing, and silent failure - it designs the exact stop conditions, budgets, and circuit-breakers the agent enforces on itself, plus a run-state counter it checks every turn.

t: Prompt-Improver Meta-Agent: Audit and Harden Any Agent Spec Before It Runs
te: A prompt-critic that diagnoses your agent/task instructions - flagging ambiguities, missing constraints, undefined stop conditions, and runaway-loop risks - then rewrites them into a tight, production-grade, runnable spec.

t: Daily Operator Agent: An Always-On Chief of Staff That Runs Your Morning
te: Stands up an always-on chief-of-staff agent that ingests your day, names the single highest-leverage move, triages the fires, drafts your urgent replies, and runs an autonomous capture-clarify-organize loop - ending with a JSON decision queue.

t: Orchestrator + Parallel Subagents That Cut Deep-Task Time
te: Generates the full lead-agent-plus-workers spec for any deep task: the orchestrator plans once, fans out isolated subagents, then synthesizes distilled summaries into one JSON contract.

t: Generator vs Evaluator Loop That Won't Ship Slop
te: Builds a two-agent quality rig: a Generator drafts, a separate Evaluator scores it against a strict rubric and feeds back, looping until it passes or hits an iteration cap.

t: ReAct Agent Backbone That Stops Hallucinated Steps
te: Writes a production ReAct system prompt that grounds the model in real tool results instead of hallucinated reasoning, looping THOUGHT / ACTION / OBSERVATION until it can answer.

t: Plan-and-Execute Pipeline That Slashes Token Cost
te: Splits any complex job into a one-time plan from a strong model and N cheap execution steps, cutting token cost versus pure ReAct on long tasks.

t: Reflexion Self-Critique Loop for a Near-Free Accuracy Lift
te: Adds a near-free accuracy bump to any agent: it critiques its own draft against criteria, stores the critique in memory, and retries only the weak spots.

t: Proposer-Skeptic-Judge Debate That Kills Yes-Man Answers
te: Stress-tests any decision by pitting a Proposer against a Skeptic who must break it, then a Judge who rules on the evidence and returns a verdict contract.

t: Schema-First Tool Surface That Stops Bad Calls
te: Designs a clean, low-failure tool surface for any agent: flat schemas, enums over free-text, a description on every property, reversibility flags, and an action-selection enum.

t: Defense-in-Depth Guardrails Against Prompt Injection
te: Specs a 5-layer guardrail architecture for tool-using agents: input guard, action gating, output guard, human approval for irreversible actions, and an injection-isolation rule.

t: External-Memory Agent That Beats Context Rot
te: Designs a long-running agent that defeats context rot by writing findings to a durable note store and compacting on a budget — so a 100-turn run stays as sharp as turn one.

t: Spec Interrogator That Kills Vibe-Coding Bugs Early
te: Inverts vibe-coding: before writing a line, the agent relentlessly interviews you one question at a time and proposes its own recommended answer so you can just say 'yes, go with that.'

t: Programmatic Tool-Calling: One Script, Not 10 Round-Trips
te: Turns a chain of dependent tool calls into one orchestration script that loops, filters, and branches in code, returning only the final result instead of flooding the context window.

t: PR Wrap-Up Subagent That Opens the Pull Request for You
te: Specs a reusable subagent that takes finished code and opens the pull request end to end — change report, tests, description, and a self-review gate — as one chained skill.

t: Meta-Prompt That Turns Any Rough Ask Into an Agent Spec
te: A prompt-engineer agent that diagnoses a vague request, surfaces ambiguities and missing constraints, then rewrites it as a clean role+task+constraints spec with a strict output contract.

t: The Brutal Mirror: Self-Reflection Coach That Names Your Blind Spots
te: A standing system prompt that turns your custom GPT or Claude Project into an unflinching reflection partner. It reads your real patterns from conversation history and memory, then names the blind spot you keep avoiding, with zero flattery.

t: Reverse-Interview Strategist: Interrogates You First, Then Nails the Deliverable
te: A reusable consultant persona that refuses generic output. It interviews you one question at a time, proposes its own best-guess answer to each so you can move at speed, and only builds once it is 90% sure it can deliver excellent work.

t: Devil's Advocate: Skeptical-Investor Persona That Tries to Kill Your Plan
te: Installs an anti-agreeable persona that stress-tests every plan like a partner-level VC who wants to pass. No validation, no cheerleading, only the strongest counterarguments and the exact risk most likely to sink you.

t: Spec-First Enforcer: Locks the Contract So Output Never Drifts Off-Brief
te: A persona that treats every request as a spec. It locks Success Criteria, Output Contract, and Constraints up front, confirms them in one compact block, then refuses to silently drift from what it agreed to deliver.

t: Claude-Native XML Architect: Builds Tagged Prompts Claude Obeys Reliably
te: Turns a Claude Project into an expert that reasons in and emits clean XML-tagged instructions, the structure Claude follows most reliably. Built-in prompt-injection defense keeps untrusted input as data, never commands.

t: Human-Voice Editor: Strips AI Slop and Em Dashes in One Pass
te: A standing editor persona that rewrites anything in one shot to read as authentically human. High burstiness, varied rhythm, banned AI tells, zero em dashes, and your meaning, facts, names, and numbers left exactly intact.

t: Prompt Doctor: Diagnoses and Rewrites Any Prompt Before You Run It
te: A meta-prompt critic that dissects your rough prompt into what is clear, what is ambiguous, and what is missing, then rewrites it into a tight, reusable spec tuned for your target model (Claude, GPT, or Gemini).

t: AI Chief of Staff: Runs Your Day From Outcomes Backward
te: An always-on operator persona that runs your day backwards from results. It names the ONE highest-leverage task, triages the fires, drafts your most urgent replies, and runs a structured Friday review on command.

t: Reflexion Engine: Self-Critiques and Rewrites Until It Scores 9+
te: A persona that never hands you a raw first draft. It generates, attacks its own work against a rubric as a hostile reviewer, rewrites every defect, and loops until each criterion clears the bar, then reports the quality gain.

t: Feynman Learning Architect: 4-Layer Teach-Back Coach That Hunts Your Gaps
te: A coach persona that explains any concept at four escalating depths, then makes YOU teach it back so it can pinpoint the exact misconception and re-teach only that, looping until you can explain it cleanly.

t: Voice-Cloning Ghostwriter: Builds Your Style Guide, Then Writes As You
te: A persona that reverse-engineers your writing voice from samples into a reusable style guide, then ghostwrites anything in that exact voice, quirks and all, so your content never reads as AI.

t: Lethal-Trifecta Guardrail: Agent Persona With a Human-Approval Gate
te: A safety-first persona for tool-using assistants. It separates untrusted input from instructions, treats fetched content as data not commands, and freezes for explicit human sign-off before any irreversible or external action.

t: Nano-Banana Image Director: Identity-Preserving Restyle Prompts That Render Text
te: A persona that writes pixel-perfect conversational prompts for Nano Banana / Gemini Image. It restyles your uploaded photo while keeping the exact same face, and quotes on-image words so the text actually renders legibly.

t: System-Prompt Auditor: Red-Teams Your Instructions and Hardens the Holes
te: A red-team persona that audits your custom GPT or Claude Project's own instructions for extraction leaks, injection holes, and weak refusals, then rewrites them to close every gap, without ever exposing your secrets.

t: Inbox-to-Action Email Agent (System Prompt + JSON)
te: Generates a production-ready system prompt for an autonomous agent that reads, classifies, and drafts replies to your inbox with built-in guardrails.

t: One Sentence to a Full n8n / Make Build Blueprint
te: Turns a one-line description of what you want automated into a full, buildable node-by-node automation blueprint with error handling and a test plan.

t: Turn GPT Into a Reliable AI Employee (Role Charter)
te: Defines a reusable AI 'employee' with a job description, SOPs, escalation rules, and KPIs so it delivers consistent work every single time.

t: Design a Coordinated AI Agent Crew (Roles & Handoffs)
te: Breaks any complex goal into a coordinated team of specialized AI agents with clear handoffs, shared memory, and a manager agent.

t: Build a Citation-Backed Support Bot That Never Makes Things Up
te: Writes the agent instructions for a knowledge-base support bot that answers only from your docs, cites every claim, and escalates cleanly.

t: Lead-Scoring Agent That Writes CRM-Ready Records
te: Specs an agent that enriches inbound leads, scores them against your ICP with a transparent rubric, and writes a clean CRM record with a next action.

t: Self-Improving Prompt Loop: Draft, Red-Team, Rewrite, Score
te: An agentic meta-prompt that drafts an answer, brutally red-teams its own output, rewrites it, and reports the measurable quality gain.

t: The Agent Builder That Interviews You First
te: A meta-agent that interrogates you about edge cases, triggers, and failure modes, then writes the optimal system prompt for your automation.

t: Support-Ticket Triage Agent (Classify, Route, Reply)
te: Designs an agent that categorizes, prioritizes, tags, and drafts the first response for every incoming support ticket with SLA-aware logic.

t: Daily Ops Digest Agent: 3 Things That Need You Today
te: Specs a scheduled agent that pulls your scattered daily data sources and ships a prioritized, skimmable 'what matters + do-next' brief.

t: Trigger-Based Cold Outreach Agent (Personalized, Anti-Spam)

t: Meeting Transcript to Owners, Deadlines & Decisions
te: Turns any meeting transcript into a 15-second executive summary plus a decisions and action-items table with owners and dates.

t: Drop-In Guardrail Block for Any AI Agent
te: Generates a reusable safety, scope, and refusal block you can append to ANY agent prompt to stop hallucinations, overreach, and prompt injection.

te: Acts as a consultant that maps your workflow, scores each step's automation ROI, and hands you a ranked, phased build roadmap.

t: Stress-Test Your AI Agent Before You Ship It
te: Generates an adversarial test suite and pass/fail rubric that hammers your agent with edge cases, jailbreaks, and failure modes before it goes live.

te: Turns your value claims into a transparent ROI model with explicit inputs, conservative assumptions, and a payback view a CFO would accept.

te: Compresses a complex deal into a one-page brief for a busy executive: the business outcome, the cost of inaction, the proposed path, and the single ask.

t: Build a Renewal Risk Plan Before the Conversation Starts

t: Spot Expansion Opportunities and Build the Land-and-Expand Path
te: Analyzes a healthy account for upsell and cross-sell openings, ranks them by fit and timing, and lays out the path to grow without risking the core relationship.

t: Map the Buying Committee and Build a Stakeholder Strategy
te: Turns a tangled enterprise org into a clear committee map — roles, influence, and stance — with a per-person plan to convert blockers and arm champions.

t: Run a Discovery Call Plan That Uncovers Real Pain and Budget
te: Builds a structured discovery plan with layered questions that surface the actual problem, its cost, the decision process, and whether it's worth pursuing.

t: Diagnose and Reframe a Stalled Enterprise Deal

te: Turns one slice of your expertise into a specific, fast-win lead magnet — with title options, outline, and the opt-in copy to capture emails.

t: Turn a Happy Client Into a Specific, Believable Testimonial

te: Turns a dense product spec into clear explainer copy a non-expert can understand, while keeping every claim defensible and compliant.

t: Pressure-Test Marketing Copy for Compliance Risk
te: Reviews finance or fintech marketing copy line by line for misleading, unsubstantiated, or risky claims before it goes live.

te: Designs the screens and microcopy for identity verification onboarding that collects what's needed, explains why, and minimizes drop-off.

te: Turns a wall of finance metrics into a tight narrative that tells leadership what changed, why it matters, and what to do next.

t: Design Transparent Pricing & Fee Disclosure Copy

te: Compresses your whole novel into a clean one-page synopsis plus a logline and elevator pitch - covering the full arc and ending, written to read as a story, not a summary.

t: One-Page Game Design Document From a Pitch
te: Turns a loose game idea into a tight one-page design doc with a core loop, player fantasy, win/lose conditions, and the single hook that makes it worth building.

t: Core Game Loop & Economy Designer
te: Designs a balanced moment-to-moment loop and resource economy - sources, sinks, currencies, and pacing - so progression feels earned and never grindy or broken.

t: Tabletop RPG Campaign Architect
te: Builds an original multi-session RPG campaign with a central conflict, escalating arcs, branching consequences, and session-ready beats your table can actually play.

t: Memorable NPC Generator (Voice, Want, Secret)
te: Creates original NPCs that feel alive - a clear want, a distinct voice, a secret, and a hook for the party - ready to drop straight into your table.

t: Quest Hook Forge (Ten Adventures From One Seed)
te: Spins one location, item, or rumor into ten distinct quest hooks, each with stakes, a twist, and a reason the party can't just walk away.

t: Branching Choose-Your-Own-Adventure Builder
te: Writes an original interactive story with numbered nodes, meaningful branches, real consequences, and multiple endings - structured so it's easy to play and easy to expand.

t: Original Trivia Pack With Difficulty Tiers
te: Generates a clean, fact-checked trivia pack on any topic with tiered difficulty, plausible wrong answers, and a one-line explainer for every question.

t: Logic & Riddle Puzzle Designer With Fair Clues
te: Designs original solvable puzzles - riddles, logic grids, lateral-thinking, or ciphers - with fair clues, a clean solution path, and graduated hints that never just give it away.

t: Escape Room Scenario & Puzzle Chain
te: Designs a themed escape room from scratch - story, a chained sequence of puzzles, props, the master solution flow, and a hint script for the game master.

t: Board / Card Game Rulebook Writer
te: Writes a clean, unambiguous rulebook for your game - setup, turn structure, edge cases, and a worked example turn - so first-time players can play without you in the room.

t: Dialogue Tree Writer for an Interactive Character
te: Writes a branching dialogue tree for a game NPC - distinct voice, player choices that reveal character, and branches that lead to real outcomes like a quest, a clue, or a fight.

t: Game Concept Critic & Fun-Finder
te: Stress-tests your game concept like a sharp publisher would - finds the fun, the fatal flaw, the comparison trap, and the cheapest prototype to prove it before you build.

t: Original Song Concept From a Single Feeling
te: Turns one emotion or moment into a fully realized original song concept: the core idea, the angle nobody else takes, a title, and the emotional arc the song will travel.

t: Full Original Lyric Writer (Verse-Chorus-Bridge)
te: Writes a complete original song lyric with verses, a memorable chorus, and a turn at the bridge - built on a real emotional arc, a consistent voice, and a hook worth repeating.

t: Hook & Chorus Engine (10 Original Options)
te: Generates ten distinct, original chorus hooks for one song idea - each with a different angle, rhythm, and emotional charge - so you can pick the one that sticks instead of settling.

t: Song Structure & Arrangement Map
te: Designs an original section-by-section structure and arrangement plan for a song - dynamics, energy curve, where to add and strip back - so the track breathes and builds.

t: Chord Progression & Mood Designer
te: Proposes original chord-progression ideas matched to a target mood, with the emotional logic behind each move, voicing ideas, and how the progression supports the lyric.

t: Genre & Reference-Vibe Translator
te: Reshapes one original song idea into a chosen genre - converting its rhythm, harmony, instrumentation, and lyrical phrasing to fit the style without copying any artist.

t: Lyric Doctor (Sharpen Weak Lines)
te: Diagnoses what's flattening your original lyric - clich, abstraction, forced rhyme, on-the-nose lines - and prescribes sharper rewrites that keep your voice and meaning intact.

t: Artist Bio & Origin Story (Press-Ready)
te: Writes an original, press-ready artist bio in three lengths - the sound, the story, the why - in your real voice, with no invented credits or fake hype.

t: Single Release Plan & Rollout Calendar
te: Builds a realistic, week-by-week rollout plan for an original single - pre-save, teaser, release day, and post-launch - mapped to what an independent artist can actually execute.

t: Topline & Melody-in-Words Sketcher
te: Sketches a singable original topline - syllable rhythm, phrasing, where notes rise and fall - described in words and simple notation so you can hum and record it fast.

t: Co-Write Pack: Brainstorm 8 Original Song Seeds
te: Acts as a co-writer and fires off eight original song seeds for a theme - each with a title, angle, hook idea, and the feeling it chases - so you never stare at a blank page.

t: Song-by-Song Album Concept & Tracklist
te: Designs an original album concept: the central idea, the emotional journey across the tracklist, how songs talk to each other, and a one-line brief for every track.

t: Episode Outline That Earns the Next 40 Minutes
te: Turns a fuzzy topic into a beat-by-beat episode map — cold-open hook, segment arcs, tension points, and a payoff close — so the recording has a spine instead of wandering.

t: Interview Question Set That Gets Past the Press-Kit Answers
te: Builds a layered question arc for a guest interview — warm-up, depth, the one risky question, and a memorable closer — designed to surface stories and specifics, not rehearsed soundbites.

t: Show Notes That Get Found, Clicked, and Remembered
te: Transforms a finished episode into complete show notes — hook summary, timestamped chapters, key takeaways, quotable lines, links, and a search-friendly title — ready to paste into any host.

t: Intro and Outro Scripts in Your Actual Voice
te: Writes a tight, repeatable cold-open + intro and a warm, action-driving outro tailored to your show's tone — so every episode starts and ends like it was made on purpose.

t: Recurring Segment Ideas That Give Your Show a Spine
te: Designs 5-8 repeatable, on-brand segments — recurring bits, listener interactions, signature closers — that make episodes feel like a show people tune into, not a stream of one-offs.

t: Guest Pitch That Books Hard-to-Get Names
te: Writes a short, flattering-but-not-fawning outreach pitch that respects a busy person's time and makes saying yes easy — with the angle, the ask, and the logistics handled.

t: Repurpose One Episode Into a Week of Content
te: Mines a single episode for its best moments and turns them into platform-native posts — audiogram clips, a thread, short captions, and a newsletter blurb — each rewritten for its channel, not copy-pasted.

t: Solo Episode Script From a Rough Idea
te: Turns a half-formed idea into a structured solo-episode script with a hook, a clear argument, stories and examples slotted in, and a strong close — written to be spoken, not read.

t: Season or Series Arc Planner
te: Designs a coherent multi-episode arc — a season theme, episode-by-episode progression, recurring threads, and a finale that pays it off — so a run of episodes feels like a series, not a pile.

t: Interview Prep Brief: Research a Guest in One Pass
te: Builds a focused pre-interview brief — what to read, the guest's likely talking points, the questions only they can answer, and the traps to avoid — so you walk in prepared, not winging it.

t: Trailer Script That Makes Strangers Subscribe
te: Writes a 60-90 second show trailer that names exactly who the podcast is for, promises a specific payoff, teases the vibe, and ends with a reason to subscribe right now — for a new show or a relaunch.

t: Audience Q&A and Listener-Mail Episode Builder
te: Turns a pile of listener questions into a structured Q&A episode — grouped themes, the best questions surfaced, punchy answer frames, and a hook to keep questions coming — instead of a flat read-through.

t: Original Screenplay Scene Writer (Industry Format)
te: Writes a fully formatted, original screenplay scene with sluglines, action lines, and dialogue that carries subtext instead of stating it.

t: Logline Forge: 7 Loglines + The Winner
te: Turns a rough story idea into 7 distinct, sellable loglines using different hooks, then names the strongest and explains why it sells.

t: TV Series Bible & Pilot Treatment Builder
te: Develops an original series concept into a mini-bible: world, characters, engine, season arc, and a pilot treatment beat by beat.

t: Sketch Comedy Script Writer (Escalating Premise)
te: Writes an original comedy sketch built on one absurd premise that escalates by 'heightening' to a hard button-line ending.

t: Dialogue Polish Pass (Voice, Subtext, Rhythm)
te: Rewrites flat dialogue so each character sounds distinct, says less, means more, and the scene's tension lands harder.

t: Shot List & Coverage Plan From a Script Page
te: Breaks a scene into a director's shot list with shot sizes, angles, movement, and coverage so a small crew can shoot it efficiently.

t: Commercial & Brand Video Script (Story-First)
te: Writes an original brand or product video script that tells a small human story, lands the message, and ends on one clear action.

t: Scene Beat Sheet From Premise to Shootable Beats
te: Turns a scene idea into an ordered beat sheet where each beat has a goal, an obstacle, and a state change - ready to write into pages.

t: Character Voice & Backstory Builder for Scripts
te: Creates a deep, original character profile - wound, want, contradictions, and a voice cheat-sheet - so their dialogue writes itself.

t: Feature Film Treatment (Three-Act Synopsis)
te: Expands a premise into a polished prose treatment with three acts, key set pieces, the midpoint turn, and the emotional throughline.

t: Short Film Script (Single Location, One Strong Idea)
te: Writes a complete original short film built around one location and one twist or idea, formatted and shootable on a small budget.

t: Script Punch-Up & Coverage Notes (Reader's Pass)
te: Reviews your script pages like a studio reader: flags structure, pacing, dialogue, and clarity problems, then gives prioritized fixes.

t: Plain English to Excel & Sheets Formula (Both Dialects)
te: Describe what you want in plain English and get the exact working formula for both Excel and Google Sheets, explained and tested.

t: Fix Any Broken Formula: #REF!, #N/A, #VALUE!
te: Paste a formula that's throwing an error or wrong result and get a line-by-line diagnosis plus the corrected, hardened version.

t: Messy Data Cleanup Playbook: Dedupe, Dates, Text
te: Turn an inconsistent, dirty dataset into clean, analysis-ready columns with step-by-step formulas and rules you run top to bottom.

t: Pivot Table Architect: From Question to Setup
te: Tell it the business question and it designs the exact pivot table - rows, columns, values, filters - with click-by-click setup.

t: Plain English to SQL Query (With Explanation)
te: Describe the data pull you need in everyday language and get a clean, dialect-correct SQL query with a line-by-line explanation.

t: One-Page KPI Dashboard Blueprint for Spreadsheets
te: Hand it your raw columns and a goal; it designs a clean one-page dashboard - KPIs, charts, layout wireframe, and the formulas.

t: Conditional Formatting Rules From Plain English
te: Describe what you want highlighted and get the exact conditional-formatting rule, formula, and applies-to range to make it happen.

t: Realistic Test Data Generator for Any Schema
te: Get realistic, ready-to-paste sample data for any schema so you can build and test formulas before real data lands.

t: Build a Macro / Apps Script to Automate the Boring Stuff
te: Describe a repetitive spreadsheet task and get ready-to-paste VBA or Google Apps Script that automates it, with setup steps.

t: Merge Two Sheets by Key (XLOOKUP / INDEX-MATCH)
te: Combining two tables by a shared key? Get the right method, the exact lookup formula, and how to catch silent mismatches.

t: Find the Story in My Data: Trends, Outliers, So-What
te: Paste a summary of your dataset and get the key trends, anomalies, and 'so what' actions a sharp analyst would surface.

t: Google Sheets QUERY & ARRAYFORMULA Power Builder
te: Get one elegant single-cell QUERY or ARRAYFORMULA that replaces dozens of dragged-down formulas and updates live.

t: Stop Bad Data at Entry: Validation & Dropdown System
te: Get dropdown lists, dependent menus, and validation rules that keep junk out of your sheet at the point of entry.

t: Regex & Text-Extraction Recipes for Spreadsheets
te: Pull emails, IDs, numbers, or any pattern out of messy text cells with ready-to-use regex and text formulas tested on your samples.

t: Replace a $60K Hire With a Documented AI-Employee System
te: Turn one chaotic business function into a documented AI-employee with a CLAUDE.md-style charter, SOPs, tool stack, escalation rules, and a daily run loop you can hand straight to an agent.

t: Rank Every Task by Hours Saved: Your Automation ROI Roadmap
te: Map your whole operation, then get a prioritized automation roadmap ranked by hours saved per week, plus a delegate/automate/keep verdict for each task and a build-ready blueprint for the top win.

t: Paste-Ready Build Blueprint for n8n, Make, or Zapier
te: Describe an automation in plain English and get a node-by-node build blueprint - triggers, modules, data mapping, filters, error handling, and a test plan - for Zapier, Make, or n8n.

t: Turn Messy Notes Into a Junior-Proof SOP You Can Delegate
te: Convert a rough chat log or brain-dump into a clean, junior-proof SOP with roles, triggers, step-by-step actions, decision rules, screenshots-to-capture, and a QA checklist - ready to hand off.

t: The One Dashboard That Tells You If the Business Is Healthy
te: Design a single command-center dashboard - the metrics that matter, their formulas, data sources, refresh cadence, alert thresholds, and layout - build-ready for Sheets, Notion, or a BI tool.

t: Support Triage System: Auto-Resolve, AI-Draft, or Human-Only
te: Feed in real support tickets and get a triage system - auto-resolve, AI-draft-for-review, and human-only buckets - plus ready-to-deploy reply templates and a routing flow.

t: The Monday Outbound Machine: One SOP You Run Every Week
te: Package research, personalization, a 5-touch cadence, and objection handling into ONE documented outbound system - a repeatable machine, not one-off prompts - that you can run weekly or sell as a service.

t: Find an AI-Runnable Business People Already Pay Experts For
te: Scan your skills and surface concrete, fundable income wedges - tasks people already pay for that you can package into an AI-runnable product this month - each with a pricing model and a red-team validation.

t: A Self-Running 30-Day Content Engine for Every Platform
te: Stand up a documented content engine - pillars, a repurposing flow, a 30-day calendar, platform-specific reformatting prompts, and a weekly run loop - so one person publishes everywhere on autopilot.

t: Stress-Test Your Plan Against 5 Hostile C-Suite Minds
te: Pressure-test a business or automation plan against a simulated executive council - CFO, COO, CMO, Head of Risk, and a hostile competitor - then get one reconciled decision with explicit kill criteria.

t: Pre-Mortem Your Automation Before It Wastes Six Months
te: Before you build, imagine the automation has already failed - and work backward to the causes, the earliest warning signals, and the guardrails that prevent each. Kills bad builds before they waste weeks.

t: 30-Day Plan to Get Out of the Weeds Without Hiring Yet
te: Map everything you personally do, then get a handoff plan: the SOPs to write, the exact tasks to automate vs delegate, a VA hire spec, and a week-by-week 30-day sequence so work leaves your plate without breaking.

t: Encode Your Best Work Into a Reusable AI Skill File
te: Turn a repeatable workflow into a permanent, versioned AI Skill / CLAUDE.md file - voice, standards, steps, examples, and guardrails - so every output ships on-standard across your team with zero re-prompting.

t: Mine Public Buying Signals Into a Scored Lead List With Angles
te: Turn free public signals - job posts, funding news, hiring sprees, website copy - into a scored lead list with a ready outreach angle per prospect, plus the agent flow to keep it running weekly.

t: Salary Negotiation War-Room: Win Thousands on the Offer Call
te: Turn one nerve-wracking call into a prepared campaign: a verified market range, your anchor/target/walk-away numbers, rehearsed objection handling, and a ready-to-send counter-offer email worth real money.

t: Promotion Business Case: Make Leadership Say Yes
te: Build the one-page promotion case your manager can forward upward unedited - evidence mapped to the next level's rubric, dollar impact quantified, gaps pre-empted, and a calm ask script for the conversation.

t: Role-Play the Hiring Manager: Live Negotiation Sparring
te: Rehearse the offer call against a realistic, slightly tough hiring manager who pushes back the way real ones do - then get graded line-by-line so you walk in unflappable.

t: Personal-Brand-to-Revenue Engine: From Followers to a Paid Offer
te: Stop posting into the void. Get the exact bridge from your audience's pain to a sellable offer, the content pillars that pre-sell it, and a 30-day plan that ends in your first paid customer - not just likes.

t: Productize My Service: 3 Tiered Offers With Anchor Pricing
te: Convert flat-rate, time-for-money work into a good/better/best ladder with anchor pricing and a one-line pitch per tier - the single move that most reliably doubles close rate and client value.

t: Freelancer-to-Agency Operating System: SOPs, Proposals & Delegation
te: The bottleneck stops being 'can I do the work' and becomes 'can I scale it.' Get the proposal template, the delegation-ready SOP, and the first-hire plan that lets you deliver like a team without becoming the team.

t: High-Income Skill Plan: Pick the Skill, Then the 90-Day Path to Paid
te: Stop collecting random tutorials. Get the one high-income skill best matched to you and the market, decomposed into trainable micro-skills, with a 90-day plan that ends in a portfolio piece and your first paid client.

t: Career Strategist: 3 Realistic Paths + a Dated 6-Month Plan
te: Trade vague ambition for an operating plan: three realistic next moves, the exact skill gaps blocking each, and a dated 6-month roadmap - the career prompt that gives a strategy, not a pep talk.

t: Reverse-Engineer the Job: Close the Gap to a Target Role in 90 Days
te: Paste the dream job description and your background. Get the precise gap between them and a 90-day plan to look like the obvious hire - so you apply from strength, not hope.
v: [WHEN I WANT TO APPLY / HOW LONG I HAVE]

t: Warm-Intro Job Engine: Skip the Black Hole, Get Referred In
te: Stop firing resumes into applicant-tracking voids where 75% are auto-rejected. Get a target-company hit list, the exact people to reach, and copy-paste outreach that turns cold strangers into the referral that lands the interview.

t: Raise Conversation Builder: Quantified Case + Calm Ask Script
te: Walk into the pay-rise talk with leverage: your contributions in dollars, a defensible target backed by market data, the answers to every 'budget is tight' deflection, and the exact sentence to open with.

t: Strategic Pre-Mortem: Stress-Test a Big Career or Income Bet
te: Before you quit, pivot, or go all-in, imagine it already failed 18 months out and work backward to the causes - a technique shown to surface failure reasons far better than a normal 'what could go wrong'.

t: Brutally Honest Career Advisor: The Truth You're Avoiding
te: Strip the AI of its default flattery and get the blind-spot-cutting feedback a $500/hr mentor gives - what's actually capping your income or career, named plainly, with a plan to fix it.

t: One-Person-Business Leverage Map: Productize a Skill, Automate the Rest
te: Find the thing people already pay experts for that you could package this month, then map which of your workflows an AI agent can run with under 20% oversight - the core solopreneur scaling move.

t: The Idea Validation Tribunal: Kill It Cheap or Greenlight It
te: A skeptical three-seat tribunal (demand realist, unit-economics auditor, distribution skeptic) interrogates your idea against falsifiable tests, then issues a GO / FIX / KILL verdict with a 7-day, sub-$200 test to run before you build anything.

t: Pre-Mortem War Room: Write the Obituary Before You Bet
te: Prospective hindsight for a discrete founder bet (pivot, raise, upmarket move). It assumes the decision already failed 18 months out, ranks the 7 most likely causes of death with the earliest warning signal for each, then converts them into a tripwire dashboard you watch from day

t: The VC Investment Memo Mirror: Read the Memo a Partner Writes About You
te: Generates the internal memo a skeptical investor would circulate about your startup — thesis, market, moat, traction, risks, and the one reason they'd pass — so you can fix the weak spots before the partner meeting instead of discovering them in the room.

t: Fundraising Narrative Architect: The Story That Survives Diligence
te: Builds your raise as a narrative, not a feature list — inevitability arc, 'why now', the wedge-to-empire path, and the proof points that defuse the objection a skeptical partner is already forming. Ends with the exact 12-slide spine and the one number that must be true.

t: The Moat Classifier: Prove Defensibility Against the 4-Moat Taxonomy
te: Diligence now weights moats at 30-40% of underwriting. This forces a specific defensibility answer — data flywheel, workflow integration, distribution surface, or network effect — strength-scores each, exposes the 'this isn't defensible' objection, and hands you the moat slide th

t: Go-To-Market Wedge Finder: The Narrowest Beachhead That Wins
te: Stops the 'we serve everyone' trap. Picks the single segment with the highest intensity-of-need and lowest CAC, names the wedge use-case, the one channel to win it, and a 90-day GTM plan — then defines the expansion path from beachhead to broader market.

t: Competitor Teardown: Reverse-Engineer a Rival's Entire Playbook
te: Reconstructs a competitor's strategy from public signals — positioning, pricing logic, which messages they push, where they spend, and the gaps they consistently ignore — then converts it into a battle card and the wedge you attack where they're weakest.

t: Board / Investor Update That Builds Trust (Not Spin)
te: Turns your raw month into a crisp, credible investor update VCs actually read: TLDR, metrics with honest context, wins, the lowlights you own before they ask, and 1-3 specific asks. Builds the trust that makes the next round — and warm intros — easier.

t: TAM/SAM/SOM With Receipts: Market Math a Skeptic Can't Dismiss
te: Builds an investor-grade, bottoms-up market size with every assumption stated and sourced — top-down AND bottoms-up, cross-checked — so your number survives the 'how did you get this?' question instead of collapsing under it.

t: Adversarial Strategy Simulator: War-Game Your Rivals' Counter-Moves
te: Casts the AI as a hostile war room. Given your plan and 2-3 rivals, it maps each competitor's most damaging response to your next move, rates the threats by severity, and hands you the pre-planned counter for the one most likely to hurt.

t: Pricing & Packaging Architect: Tiers, Anchors, and the Number That Closes
te: Designs a value-anchored pricing model — three tiers with a deliberate decoy, the metric you charge on, and psychological anchoring — then justifies the number so you stop discounting on instinct. Ends with the single test to run before you change a live price.

t: The Brutally Honest Co-Founder: Diagnose What's Actually Holding You Back
te: Strips the AI of flattery and turns it into the blunt co-founder you need — it diagnoses the real bottleneck (you, the model, the market, or the focus), names the uncomfortable truth you're avoiding, and gives a hard 30-day plan to break the pattern.

t: Jobs-To-Be-Done Synthesizer: Turn Raw Customer Talk Into a Demand Map
te: Decomposes interviews, reviews, and sales-call notes into functional, emotional, and social jobs in the buyer's own words, flags which jobs are well-served vs. wide open, and converts the gaps into a prioritized opportunity map you can build and message against.

t: 90-Day Founder Operating System: From Strategy to a Cadence You Run
te: Converts a fuzzy quarter into a disciplined operating system: one north-star metric, 3 ranked objectives, the weekly cadence that enforces them, the 20% of work protected first, and the explicit list of what you'll deliberately NOT do this quarter.

t: The 80/20 Mastery Map: Skip 80% of a Skill, Keep the Power
te: Strips a whole domain down to the 20% of concepts that produce 80% of real-world competence, then sequences them into a brutal, no-fluff curriculum with a first-week plan you can start today.

t: Deliberate-Practice Engine: Turn a Skill Into Daily Drills That Actually Move You
te: Breaks any complex skill into the smallest trainable micro-components, then designs a timed daily drill for each with an objective pass-bar and a built-in feedback loop, so reps stop being busywork.

t: The 90-Day Skill Sprint: From Beginner to Provable in One Quarter
te: A complete dated 90-day operating system that works backward from a final proof-of-skill deliverable - weekly milestones, a rising project ladder, named feedback sources, and quit-proofing for the 3 ways people drop out.

t: Decision Cockpit: Run Any Big Call Through 6 Elite Thinking Tools
te: Puts a real decision through inversion, first principles, second-order effects, base rates, opportunity cost, and circle of competence - surfacing the failure modes and hidden assumptions a friendly advisor would never mention.

t: Build Your Personal Latticework: The 12-16 Mental Models Your Job Actually Needs
te: Designs a custom curriculum of the highest-leverage mental models for YOUR field and recurring decisions, with power-pairings, a worked example on your real situation, and a 5-minute weekly drill to wire each one in.

t: Pre-Mortem War Room: Kill Your Plan on Paper Before Reality Does
te: Travels months into the future, declares your plan a failure, and reconstructs the causal chain backward - then hands you the earliest warning signals to monitor so the failure never actually happens.

t: First-Principles Teardown: Rebuild Any Belief or Plan From Bedrock
te: Strips a belief, plan, or 'best practice' down to its irreducible truths, exposes which assumptions are inherited by convention vs. actually proven, then reconstructs the cheapest, strongest version from scratch.

t: Feynman Coach: Understand It, Then Prove You Can Use It
te: Explains any concept at your exact level with one clean analogy, then refuses to move on until you teach it back and pass a retrieval quiz - closing the 'I read it but can't actually use it' gap with a spaced re-test schedule.

t: Strategy-Coach Interrogation: Questions First, Advice Last
te: Forces the model to interview you like a $500/hr strategy coach - surfacing your real constraints, hidden assumptions, and the actual problem - before it's allowed to recommend anything, so you don't get a confident answer to the wrong question.

t: The Brutally Honest Advisor: Find the Real Thing Capping Your Growth
te: Strips away the flattery and names the uncomfortable truth about what's actually plateauing you - the avoided skill, the comfortable busywork, the excuse dressed as a reason - then pairs every hard truth with a 30-day corrective plan.

t: Plateau-Breaker: Diagnose Why You're Stuck and Re-Sequence Your Practice
te: Pinpoints exactly why your progress has flatlined - autopilot reps, wrong difficulty, or a broken feedback loop - and rebuilds your practice routine to force the next level of growth, with a 2-week test to confirm the breakthrough.

t: Knowledge-to-Action: Convert Courses & Notes You've Already Consumed Into Skill
te: Takes the books, courses, and notes you've passively consumed but never used, extracts the few ideas worth keeping, and forges them into retrieval prompts and real-world action triggers so the knowledge finally pays off.

t: Skill-Transfer Architect: Compound What You Already Master Into a New One
te: Maps the deep structure of a skill you've already mastered onto a new domain you're learning - so you start the new skill from your existing expertise instead of from zero, while flagging where old instincts will sabotage you.

t: Quarterly Mastery OS: Audit Last Quarter, Pick 3 Goals, Protect the Calendar
te: A recurring operating system for growth - a brutal audit of where your effort actually went, three prioritized skill goals with day-90 proofs, a defended weekly cadence, and 1%-rule habits that compound into mastery.

t: Install a Personal Operating System That Runs Your Life on Autopilot
te: Stop managing your life from your inbox. Install a complete personal OS — capture, prioritize, time-block, and weekly review — engineered around the 20% of actions that drive 80% of your results.

t: Audit Where Your Hours Bleed Out and Reclaim 8-15 a Week
te: A forensic audit of where your time and energy actually go — scored by ROI — so you can cut, automate, delegate, or protect each activity. Most people recover 8-15 hours a week.

t: Stop Being the Bottleneck: Decide What to Hand Off and Hand It Off Clean
te: The reason you're the bottleneck isn't workload — it's that nothing is documented. This builds your delegation decision matrix AND the SOP for the first task, so handoffs stop boomeranging back.

t: Find Your Highest-Multiplier Move (Code · Content · Capital · Labor)
te: Naval's four levers, made concrete for your situation. Audit where you currently get leverage, where you're trading time for money, and the single highest-multiplier move to make next.

t: Turn Daily 1% Inputs Into Exponential 12-Month Gains
te: Big goals fail at the daily level. This converts one ambition into the tiny, trackable inputs that compound — with a habit-stack, a scoreboard, and the math that shows where you'll be in 12 months.

t: Build a Reusable AI Employee That Runs Your Workflow Without Re-Prompting
te: Stop re-prompting the same task. Turn one repeatable workflow into a permanent, versioned instruction file — your AI 'employee' that runs on-standard every session with zero re-explaining.

t: Map What an AI Agent Can Run With Under 20% of You
te: Map every workflow in your business or life, then rank what an AI agent can run end-to-end with under 20% human oversight — so one person operates like a team without hiring.

t: Get the $500/Hr Diagnosis of Why You're Actually Stuck
te: Not a cheerleader — a $500/hr operator who refuses to flatter you. It names the avoidance pattern, the wrong-priority you're hiding behind 'busy', and the one hard change that unlocks everything.

t: Triage Every Incoming Yes/No So Your Calendar Stops Filling Itself
te: Every opportunity, favor, and meeting request is a withdrawal from your time. This installs a scoring filter that gives each request an instant accept / decline / counter — plus the exact words to say it.

t: Reclaim 10 Hours a Week From Your Inbox and Reactive Work
te: Your inbox is a to-do list other people wrote. Install triage rules, reply templates, and decision-rules that pre-make the recurring calls — so you process messages in two short batches a day, not all day.

t: Build the One Dashboard That Replaces Your 'How Am I Doing?' Anxiety

te: Turns a destination, trip length, and traveler type into a realistic day-by-day itinerary that accounts for transit, opening hours, and energy levels, so nobody ends up sprinting between sights or stranded at a closed door.

t: Write a Tour Package Description That Sells the Feeling, Not Just the Stops
te: Transforms a dry list of inclusions into a vivid, honest tour or package description that helps the right traveler picture themselves there and click book, while setting accurate expectations.

t: Send a Booking Confirmation That Answers Every Question Before They Ask
te: Turns the raw details of a confirmed booking into a clear, reassuring confirmation message that covers what they booked, what happens next, and what to bring, so your inbox stays quiet and travelers arrive prepared.

t: Write a Local Guide to a Neighborhood Like a Resident, Not a Brochure

t: Reply to a Glowing Review in a Way That Wins the Next Customer
te: Turns a happy guest's positive review into a warm, specific public reply that thanks them genuinely and quietly sells your strengths to every future traveler reading it.

t: Respond to a Harsh Travel Review Without Sounding Defensive
te: Turns a critical or unfair review into a calm, professional public reply that takes responsibility where it's due, corrects the record where needed, and reassures future travelers — protecting your reputation instead of inflaming it.

t: Craft a Pre-Trip Email That Builds Excitement and Cuts No-Shows
te: Generates a warm pre-departure email sent a few days before travel that gets people packed, prepared, and pumped, reducing last-minute cancellations and day-of confusion.

t: Pitch the Perfect Upgrade or Add-On Without Being Pushy
te: Turns a traveler's existing booking into a tailored, genuinely useful upsell offer — a room upgrade, an excursion, an experience add-on — framed around their trip so it feels like a tip from a friend, not a sales pitch.

t: Design a Multi-City Route That Minimizes Backtracking and Burnout
te: Takes a list of places a traveler wants to hit and sequences them into a sane multi-city route with logical transit, smart base-city choices, and the right number of nights per stop, so the trip flows instead of zig-zagging.

t: Write a Cancellation or Disruption Message That Keeps Goodwill Intact
te: Turns a tour cancellation, weather closure, or schedule disruption into a clear, empathetic message that explains what happened, what you're doing about it, and the traveler's options — so a bad moment doesn't become a lost customer.

t: Build a Themed Itinerary Around One Passion (Food, History, Nature)
te: Takes a destination and a single obsession and designs a focused themed itinerary — every stop, meal, and detour chosen to deepen that one interest — so a passionate traveler gets a trip built around what they actually love.

t: Turn Customer Questions Into a Clear, Trust-Building FAQ Section
te: Converts the questions travelers keep asking — and the ones they're afraid to ask — into a crisp, honest FAQ for a tour, stay, or package that reduces hesitation, cuts repetitive inquiries, and pre-empts disputes.

t: 7-Day No-Repeat Meal Plan + Aisle-Sorted Grocery List
te: Turns your diet, budget, and cooking time into a full week of no-repeat dinners with a shopping list sorted by store aisle.

t: Day-by-Day Trip Itinerary Tuned to How You Travel

t: Weekend Declutter Sprint Plan You'll Actually Finish

t: Gift Finder That Actually Nails the Person

t: Stress-Free Party Plan: Timeline, Menu & Run-of-Show
te: Plans an entire event end-to-end with a countdown timeline, scaled food quantities, and a day-of schedule.

t: DIY Project Planner With an Honest DIY-vs-Pro Verdict
te: Turns 'I want to do X myself' into a real plan with a tool list, cost estimate, and a blunt difficulty rating.

t: Tonight's Dinner From What's Already In Your Fridge

te: Builds a repeatable weekly home-management routine so chores, meals, and errands never snowball again.

t: Multi-City Trip Logistics Solver: Routes, Bookings, Buffers

t: Sinking-Fund Plan to Cover a Big Expense Without Debt

te: Gives you a timed, priority-ordered cleaning sprint that makes your place look great before company arrives.

te: Turn your scattered day into a deliberate sequence of anchors and blocks - built around your real energy curve, not a productivity guru's 5am fantasy. Get a morning, midday and evening routine you can sustain.

t: 30-Day Habit Streak Engine: A Day-by-Day Plan That Bends, Not Breaks
te: Get a structured 30-day on-ramp for one habit - escalating just enough, with built-in recovery for missed days and a 'never miss twice' rule baked in. A streak designed to survive real life.

t: Environment Designer: Make the Right Choice the Easy One
te: Stop fighting temptation with willpower. Re-engineer your home, phone and workspace so good habits sit one step closer and bad ones one step farther - the lazy person's path to discipline.

t: Deep Work System: Build the Focus Muscle Back From Scratch
te: Reclaim the ability to concentrate. Get a graduated focus-training plan, a distraction-blocking protocol, and a repeatable deep-work ritual that pulls you into the zone on command instead of waiting for the mood.

t: Accountability System Builder: Make Quitting Cost Something
te: Design the external structure that keeps you going when motivation dies - a partner cadence, public commitment, stakes and check-ins - matched to your psychology so it actually grips instead of getting ignored.

t: Habit System Diagnostic: Find Why Your Habits Keep Dying
te: Run a full forensic on your stalled habit attempts. Pinpoint the exact failure point - cue, friction, reward, identity, or environment - and get the targeted fix, instead of restarting the same broken approach.

t: Habit Stacking Sequencer: Chain Small Habits Into a Routine That Runs Itself
te: Bolt new behaviors onto things you already do without thinking. Get an optimized chain where each habit triggers the next, so the whole sequence fires automatically once you start the first link.

t: Discipline Without Motivation: A System for the Days You Don't Feel It
te: Build the protocols that carry you when willpower is empty - pre-made decisions, a non-negotiables floor, an if-then plan for every excuse, and a 'show up at 40%' standard that keeps the chain alive.

t: Weekly Habit Review Ritual: A 20-Minute System Audit That Compounds
te: Turn habit-building from a guess into a feedback loop. Run a structured weekly review that scores adherence, finds what's drifting, and resets next week's plan - the maintenance ritual that keeps systems alive long-term.

t: Identity-First Habit Shift: Become the Person, Then the Habits Follow
te: Stop chasing outcomes and start casting votes for a new self. Translate the person you want to be into the small daily proofs that build that identity - the deepest, most durable layer of habit change.

t: Sunday Batch-Cook Blueprint

t: Room-by-Room Declutter Sprint

t: Whole-Home Cleaning System Builder
te: Stop deep-cleaning in panic before guests. Build a rotating system that keeps the place handled.

t: Weeknight Meal Plan on a Budget

t: Home Project Plan & Materials List

t: Monthly Household Budget Builder
te: Turn messy income and bills into a clear monthly plan that tells your money where to go.

te: Hand over your week's meals and get one consolidated, aisle-sorted shopping list with nothing forgotten.

te: Build a freezer full of ready-to-heat meals in one cooking marathon, labeled and rotation-ready.

t: Family Command Center & Weekly Plan
te: One weekly system that wrangles everyone's schedules, meals, chores, and the never-ending to-do list.

t: Guided Journaling Session: Write Your Way to the Real Issue
te: Most journaling circles the surface. This runs you through a five-layer prompt sequence that peels past the story you tell yourself down to the actual thing underneath - then leaves you with one clear sentence and one next move.

t: Cognitive Reframe Engine: Rewrite the Story That's Hurting You
te: Take the thought looping in your head and run it through a structured distortion check - then get three sturdier ways to see the same facts, plus the one reframe you'd actually believe at 2am. Not toxic positivity; a more accurate story.

te: Run a real weekly review, not a vague 'how did it go.' Audit your wins, energy leaks, dropped balls and quiet drift - then walk out with three priorities and one thing you'll deliberately stop doing. Built for people, not project managers.

t: Gratitude Practice That Doesn't Feel Fake: Specific, Earned, Real
te: Skip the bland 'grateful for my health' list. This pulls out three specific, sensory, surprising things from your actual day - and traces each to the person or choice behind it - so gratitude lands as something felt, not performed.

t: Stress Reset Protocol: From Spinning Out to Steady in 10 Minutes
te: When your chest is tight and your mind is a browser with 40 tabs, this walks you through a fast nervous-system reset, a quick triage of what's actually yours to handle, and the single next action that stops the spiral. Practical, not preachy.

t: Setback Recovery Plan: Turn the Thing That Knocked You Down Into a Map Forward
te: After a real loss - a failed launch, a rejection, a relapse, a blow-up - this helps you feel it honestly, extract the actual lesson without self-flagellation, and rebuild momentum with one small win. No 'everything happens for a reason' nonsense.

t: Confidence Builder: Evidence-Based Self-Belief, Not Hype
te: Real confidence isn't a pep talk - it's a stack of evidence you've stopped looking at. This builds a personal proof file of times you handled hard things, then turns it into a grounded pre-event script for the moment you need it most.

t: Inner Critic Dialogue: Negotiate With the Voice in Your Head
te: That harsh inner voice isn't your enemy - it's a clumsy bodyguard. This stages a written dialogue where you interview your inner critic, find what it's actually trying to protect, and renegotiate the terms so it works for you instead of against you.

t: Values Excavation: Find the 5 Words You Want to Live By
te: Most people drift because they've never named what they're steering toward. This digs through your peak moments, your anger, and your envy to surface your actual core values - then pressure-tests them and turns them into decision filters you can use.

t: Comparison Trap Reset: Get Off the Scoreboard You Never Chose to Play On
te: Scrolling left you feeling behind and small? This untangles whose game you're actually measuring yourself against, exposes the hidden costs you're not seeing in their 'wins', and re-anchors you to your own scoreboard - the one that matters.

t: Letter From Your Future Self: Get Advice From Who You're Becoming
te: Stuck in a decision or a rough patch? This writes you a grounded, specific letter from your wiser self five years out - someone who already lived through this and knows which worries were noise. Honest counsel, not a fortune cookie.

t: Self-Compassion Reset: Treat Yourself Like Someone You're Responsible For
te: When you've messed up and the self-attack is brutal, this interrupts the spiral with a structured self-compassion practice - naming the pain, normalizing it, and speaking to yourself the way you'd speak to someone you love. Backbone, not bubble bath.

t: Build a Zero-Based Monthly Budget From Your Real Numbers
te: Turns your take-home pay and actual spending into a give-every-dollar-a-job budget that fits how you really live, not a fantasy spreadsheet you'll abandon by week two.

t: Design a Debt-Payoff Plan: Avalanche vs Snowball, Side by Side

t: Reverse-Engineer a Savings Goal Into a Monthly Number
te: Takes a goal you've only ever felt as a vague 'someday' and turns it into a specific monthly amount, a target date, and a fallback if life squeezes the plan.

t: Audit Your Subscriptions and Recurring Charges for Quiet Leaks
te: Sorts every recurring charge into keep, downgrade, pause, or cancel using a value test, then totals the realistic monthly and yearly money you'd reclaim.

t: Pressure-Test a Big Purchase Before You Buy
te: Runs a major buy through cost-per-use, opportunity cost, and a regret check so you decide on logic instead of the warm glow of wanting it.

t: Map Where Your Money Actually Goes (Spending X-Ray)
te: Takes a messy month of transactions and turns it into a clean breakdown by category and need-vs-want, exposing the gap between where you think money goes and where it actually does.

t: Plan an Emergency Fund Sized to Your Real Life
te: Calculates the cushion that actually fits your obligations and risk, then lays out a month-by-month path to build it without stalling your other goals.

t: Allocate a Raise, Bonus, or Windfall on Purpose
te: Stops a one-time windfall or new raise from quietly evaporating by splitting it across debt, savings, and guilt-free spending with a clear rationale for each slice.

t: Compare Two Money Options Without Getting Fooled by the Sticker Price
te: Puts two financial choices on equal footing by surfacing the all-in cost, the hidden fees, and the long tail so the cheaper-looking option doesn't quietly win on a technicality.

t: Build a Bill Calendar and Cash-Flow Map for the Month
te: Lines up every payday against every due date so you can see exactly which weeks run tight, where an overdraft could hit, and which bills to shift to smooth the month out.

t: Run a Quarterly Money Review and Set the Next 90 Days
te: Gives your finances the same structured review a team gives a project: what worked, what slipped, what changed, and three concrete money moves for the next quarter.

t: Negotiate a Bill Down: Script and Strategy
te: Builds a calm, specific script and fallback plan to lower a recurring bill, so you walk into the call with leverage and exact words instead of hoping they'll just be nice.

t: Hard Conversation Rehearsal: Script the Talk You've Been Avoiding
te: Turn the conversation you keep dreading into a calm, planned opening - a clear ask, the lines that will trigger you, and what to say when it goes sideways. Walk in prepared, not braced for war.

t: The Real Apology Builder: Repair, Not Just 'Sorry If'

t: Boundary Scripts: Say No Without the Guilt Spiral

t: Dating Profile Doctor: A Bio That Sounds Like a Real Person

t: First-Message Crafter: Openers That Get a Reply
te: Stop sending 'hey' into the void. Get three tailored opening messages built off something specific in their profile - curious, light, and easy to answer - so you actually start a conversation instead of getting ghosted.

t: Conflict De-Escalator: Turn a Fight Into a Conversation
te: You're mid-argument and it's spiraling. Get a fast read on what's really going on underneath, the line that lowers the temperature, and a path from attack-and-defend to actually solving the thing.

t: Stay-In-Touch System: Keep the Friendships You Keep Losing
te: Stop letting good friendships quietly fade because life got busy. Build a light, low-guilt system - a tiered list, a realistic cadence, and ready-to-send check-ins - that keeps your people close without feeling like a chore.

t: Tone Translator: Rewrite the Message Before You Hit Send
te: Paste the heated, cold, or passive-aggressive text you're about to send and get it back rewritten - same point, clear and human - plus a read on how it would actually land if you sent it as-is.

t: The Breakup / Ending Talk: End It With Honesty and Care
te: Whether it's a relationship, a friendship, or a fading situationship, get a clear, kind way to end it - what to say, what not to over-explain, and how to hold the line if they push back, without cruelty or false hope.

t: Receiving Hard Feedback: Hear It Without Getting Defensive
te: Someone close just told you something hard about yourself - and your instinct is to defend, explain, or shut down. Get a way to actually hear it, separate the true part from the unfair part, and respond like the bigger person.

t: Reconnect After Falling Out: Reopen a Door Without Groveling
te: There's someone you miss but the relationship broke or faded after a fight, a slight, or just silence. Get a way to reach back out that acknowledges what happened, doesn't grovel or re-litigate, and makes it easy for them to say yes.

t: Toast & Tribute Writer: Words for the Moments That Matter
te: Write the wedding toast, eulogy, anniversary note, or thank-you that you've been staring at a blank page over. Get something specific, heartfelt, and true to your voice - that honors the person without cliches or a wall of inside jokes.

t: Messy Text to Clean Structured Rows (Zero-Hallucination)

t: Invoice to Line Items with Auto-Verified Totals
te: Paste any invoice, receipt, or order confirmation and get a clean split: header fields, one record per line item, and a totals block the model independently re-adds and checks against the printed total - so you catch parsing errors before they hit your books.

te: Turn paragraph-soup specs, PRDs, or RFPs into a numbered traceability matrix - each requirement isolated, typed, prioritized, and given a testable acceptance criterion - plus a list of gaps and contradictions to resolve. Spec-first discipline in one prompt.

te: Merge several overlapping sources - notes, exports, pasted lists, email threads - into ONE deduplicated master table, with conflicting values flagged (not silently overwritten) and the contributing source named for each record. The cleanest merge you'll do all week.

t: Bulletproof Entity Extractor: Messy Text to Schema-Locked JSON
te: Pull people, orgs, dates, money, and links out of any chaotic text into one parser-safe JSON object that matches an exact schema - nulls for anything missing, zero hallucinated values.

t: Git Diff to Release-Ready Changelog JSON

te: Paste any unstructured mess plus the JSON shape you want, and get data perfectly coerced to fit - typed, normalized, and wrapped with a full report of what was filled, what was missing, and every coercion made.

t: Aspect-Based Sentiment Engine: Reviews to Actionable JSON
te: Slice any review or comment into per-aspect sentiment with scores, verbatim evidence, key phrases, churn-risk flags, and an overall verdict - one validated JSON object your analytics can act on.

t: Survey Open-Ends to Coded Themes: Analysis-Ready JSON

te: Paste broken, truncated, or wrong-typed JSON plus the schema it must satisfy, and get back a fixed, fully valid object with a precise log of every repair - trailing commas, unbalanced brackets, bad types, missing keys, all fixed.

t: Meeting Transcript to Decisions & Action-Items JSON

te: Turn any raw product description, spec dump, or scraped listing into one clean, SEO-ready e-commerce JSON record - title, attributes, variants, bullet features, category, and meta tags - normalized for your storefront or feed.

te: Feed in sample records or a description of your data, and get a rigorous JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) back - typed, with required fields, enums, formats, and constraints inferred - plus a plain-English rule summary you can hand to any team.

t: Plan Your Week Backwards From Results (Outcome-First OS)

te: Turn the model's memory of you into a sharp, specific read of the patterns that quietly sabotage you, then hand you the one fix that matters.

t: Make Any Hard Decision: The AI Interviews You First
te: For any hard call, the AI interviews you one question at a time, guesses your answer so you can just confirm, then builds a weighted framework and a verdict.

t: Stress-Test Your Plan Like a Skeptic Who Wants It to Fail

t: Life Audit: Where Your Time Betrays Your Priorities
te: Map where your hours actually go against what you say matters most, then get the single biggest gap surfaced with a 30-day correction.

t: Your AI Chief of Staff: A Daily Brief That Names the ONE Thing
te: Drop in your calendar, threads and projects and get a blunt morning brief that names the single highest-leverage move, drafts your two hardest replies, and tells you what to drop.

t: Friday Weekly Review That Turns Last Week's Mess Into a Plan

t: Actually Understand Anything: The Feynman 4-Layer Teach-Back

t: Build a Habit That Survives Your Worst Day (Not Just Your Best)

t: Schedule Your Day by Energy, Not the Clock

t: Turn One Big Goal Into a 90-Day Roadmap (Backwards-Planned)

t: Reclaim Your Focus: A Personal Deep-Work & Distraction Defense Plan
te: Diagnose exactly what's shredding your attention, then get a custom deep-work protocol, an environment redesign, and a single-task focus ritual.

t: Build Your Second Brain: A PARA Knowledge System That Compounds
te: Stand up a personal knowledge system that ingests your notes and sources, files them by PARA, links them, and surfaces fresh connections instead of letting them rot.

t: Research Plan Architect: Approve the Brief Before Burning a 200-Source Run
te: Turn any vague question into a reviewable research plan before a single source is read: sub-questions, source priorities, recency rules, a contradiction protocol, and an output contract you approve.

t: Competitor Teardown That a CEO Can Act On This Week

t: McKinsey-Grade Strategic Decision Report in One Prompt
te: Packages a high-end consulting engagement into one prompt: it asks 3 context questions, then runs a 9-phase pipeline applying real frameworks to deliver an executive-ready recommendation, 3 options, and a 90-day roadmap.
